entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Agile Implementation using Cloud ALM
entry.content: Hi Sri, sorry, I am not aware if there is an SAP SuccessFactors starter system or not. Best regards, Cay

entry.title: Re: Budget Transfer Timing for STO Transactions
entry.content: Thank you for your suggestion! I’m currently looking into it and will get back to you soon.

entry.title: Re: Success Factors Employee Central - set 'Show More' as...
entry.content:
Hi

Before turning on the people profile this was easily accessible, but have not found a workaround after the update on People Profile and the also Configure People Profile.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@rosenpetrovIMO if you have trouble distinguishing between a local variable and a class member your problem isn't solved by HN but by choosing better names in general.

Silly example but think about this:
Within a methode, the lifetime of a variable is very short, someone could say it's temporary, so why not use sales_orders for the class member variable and temp_customer_sales for the local one? Yea i know temp_ is basically a malformed HN but I hope you get the idea 😉 .

entry.title: Can Employee Central be configured to show all info expanded by default?
entry.content: After updating to the new People Profile, sections that were fully visible before now require clicking 'Show More.' Is there a way to configure Employee Central to display all information fully expanded by default? I’ve checked Configure People Profile but couldn’t find an option. Any advice?

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
Easy way out: Use "me->" if you need to make it clear.

me->sales_orders = sales_orders.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@rosenpetrov 

How you distinguish between class member table sales_orders and local table sales_orders ? mt_ and lt_ gives you immediate sign - local or class member variable, so i know that i'm modifying a local variable and not the class member...

Personally I use me-> or classname=> for accessing class members.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
@StephanZiemerTHIS TBH!

C# uses this, VB.NET uses Me, ABAP has the me keyword

I haven't done OOP ABAP, but I have read some OOP code. Totally forgot about that 😅

entry.title: Customize with confidence on the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In my conversations with customers, I keep hearing that extensibility is extremely important to them. It allows their systems to adapt and grow with their businesses and enables customization to fit specific needs and easy integration with other tools, enhancing overall efficiency and user experience. With SAP BTP, we are providing a stable and reliable way to keep innovating and expanding your SAP solution beyond its standard capabilities. This year at SAP TechEd, we announced the general availability of the new SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
 
The Extensibility Wizard is a tool that helps you quickly and easily add new features or customize the standard capabilities of your SAP software to better fit your business needs, without requiring extensive technical expertise.

In this blog, I’ll recap what we shared at SAP TechEd 2024 around the Extensibility Wizard, its main business value for customers, and provide some key resources to help you get started. I’ll also share my thoughts about how this release fits into SAP’s vision for extensibility and reveal a sneak peek at what lies ahead.

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard at TechEd 2024

 

 

 

 

 

The Extensibility Wizard was among this year’s key announcements at SAP TechEd. During the keynote, we also shared the news that ABAP environments are now part of SAP Build, and that customers can develop ERP extensions within a single suite of AI-driven tools. The Extensibility Wizard takes this a step further by bringing the ABAP and non-ABAP worlds together directly within the ERP, making SAP Build the default solution to modernize your core system. See all of this year’s main announcements in the TechEd keynote replay: SAP TechEd 2024 Keynote - Where ideas get real.

In case you missed it, I highly recommend checking out these on-demand sessions featuring the Extensibility Wizard, including strategy, common scenarios, demos and more:

  • AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build
  • AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions

What’s the main business value of the Extensibility Wizard?

From SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 onwards, the wizard is now available in every business application that is enabled for key user adaptation. Both key users and developers can launch the wizard directly from their SAP S/4HANA system to start creating on-stack extensions, as well as side-by-side extensions with BTP and SAP Build. In the future, we’ll plan to enable it in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud and on-prem.

See these blogs to catch up on what’s available and how to get started:

  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
  • The Extensibility Wizard: One Tool to Rule Them All?

 

 

 

So with the Extensibility Wizard readily available, what’s the business value customers can now achieve? Here are the main benefits to know:

  • All extensibility options brought together in one place: The Extensibility Wizard consolidates on-stack extensions that are already possible like custom field and logic, together with side-by-side – bringing BTP and S/4HANA Cloud together in a single extensibility space.
  • Contextualized experience for extension development: One of the wizard’s main capabilities is that it automatically understands the business context of the application you are using and filters the corresponding extension points, such as APIs and events.  For on-stack extensions, it navigates you to the respective business application or key user extensibility apps.
  • Leverages generative AI and other BTP innovations: By providing seamless navigation into SAP Build, now customers and partners can take advantage of the latest BTP capabilities, such as AI agents and prebuilt content, to truly accelerate their ERP modernization.

SAP Build as part of SAP’s overall vision for extensibility

With the release of Extensibility Wizard and the addition of ABAP environments to SAP Build, we are realizing our vision for more accessible extensibility and clean core development.

As the graphic below illustrates, SAP Build provides an entire suite of extensibility options to meet every type of developer persona or business need. From low- to pro-code, backend to frontend development – all of them are under the umbrella of SAP Build.

Just as SAP Build has made app development accessible to citizen developers, the Extensibility Wizard now makes extension creation a smoother and more streamlined experience that is clean core by default.

It guides key users through the first steps, making it easier to start and know which tools to use. Professional developers no longer need to start from scratch, as they can leverage features like gen AI and maintained, prebuilt content. SAP Build continues to enable fusion development from multiple angles, making it a truly holistic portfolio for extensibility.

Want to learn more about our vision for extensibility?

  • See five use cases of SAP Build extending SAP S/4HANA for finance, supply chain and other lines of business in the eBook.
  • Explore our overall strategy for clean core and extensibility model in this whitepaper.

Looking ahead to a new Extensibility era

With the Extensibility Wizard and our other TechEd milestones, we’re thrilled to take extensibility into a new era, where the worlds of BTP, S/4HANA, and ABAP come closer together! Developers and business users alike will be able to extend and innovate their core systems based on their specific skill sets and custom needs. And it’s only the beginning.

This visual illustrates how SAP Build encompasses the development of enterprise-ready extensions. You’ll note that generative AI is a centerpiece of the portfolio, with many new capabilities announced at TechEd to further accelerate the development process. AI is already changing how we interact with technology and develop software, and it’s a trend that will continue to shape the entire industry.

So, what’s next for the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard?

Currently, it’s possible to create custom fields, logic, and process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The option to create custom apps with SAP Build Code is on the roadmap.

We’re also pleased to announce that the Extensibility Wizard is targeted for SAP SuccessFactors in the future, offering a more robust and standardized way to enhance core HR systems. The Extensibility Wizard will operate with the same behavior and framework as it does in SAP S/4HANA, integrating SAP Build within Employee Center with numerous event hooks available.

For a sneak peek at how this will look, check out the announcement from this year’s SAP SuccessConnect event in Lisbon by Siva Sundaresan, SVP of Applications Engineering at SAP SuccessFactors. He also shares a demo of the Extensibility Wizard in the Onboarding application: Road Map and Vision Session (starts around 15:45).

***

Hopefully this got you inspired and ready to kick off your transformation journey with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard. There are plenty more capabilities to come, so stay tuned!

For additional learning, see these Devtoberfest sessions and demos:

  • S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard
  • What software developers want to know about Key User Extensibility
  • Demo - Easily extend SAP S/4HANA to create clean, core compliant extensions using the extensibility wizard
  • Demo - GenAI and SAP Build: Accelerate Development Projects

entry.title: Re: Introducing Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Monitoring in PP/DS for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Really helpful and well summarised. Great work!

entry.title: Background Job output (Report)
entry.content:
Hello All,

I’m reaching out to inquire about a solution to my issue with the background data in our report. Currently, the data appears across two or multiple rows, which disrupts the alignment and overall readability of the report.

I have attached an image for your reference.

Could you please advise if there is a way to ensure that the data consolidates into a single row? Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Field "Ship-to Party's Customer Reference Date" in SD...
entry.content:
Hi @ciskoy,

Unfortunately, this field VBKD-BSTDK_E is not exposed in the released CDS view, which means you are also not able to get the value of this field in custom logic. The current workaround is to create a custom field and manually enter the same date into it.

We don't have documentation for mapping the technical name. Basically, all field names follow the Global Field Name. (Also, the technical name is not user-friendly for the"non-SAPer" 😞 ).

If you need the field "Ship-to Party's Customer Reference Date" (VBKD-BSTDK_E) via standard Data Source Extension, please consider raising a new feature request (2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me).

Best regards,

Troy

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content:
Not using HN is only possible in combination with "Clean Code".

"Clean Code" describes the length of a method as "short" (indeed very short!). You shoud see whether a variable is local or a class attribute by simply looking at the code.

METHOD methodname.
  DATA(today) = sy-datlo.
  tomorrow = today + 1.
ENDMETHOD.

 (and we don't have to talk about global variables because you don't need to use them at all).

entry.title: Error accessing CDS View
entry.content:
An error when accessing the following CDS View to get the due date.

 

  • I_FinTransCondition
  • I_FinTransCndnSingleDate

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error accessing CDS View
entry.content: Refer SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3549623

entry.title: Re: How to automate SAP BTP Platform User management?
entry.content:
Hi Sunil,

to provide CF roles to platform users the only option is to use CF CLI command. However, if you do now want to develop your own wrapper API layer you can leverage the Automation Pilot service and its APIs. Automation Pilot will execute the commands and can be trigger via API. See https://help.sap.com/docs/automation-pilot/automation-pilot/assigncforgrole-command for details.

Kind Regards,

Matteo

entry.title: IAM Information System App – your one-stop shop for identity and administrative management queries
entry.content:
Have you ever faced challenges as an Administrator trying to identify which applications a specific Business user can access, determine how many business users are linked to a particular application, or analyse which fields have 'write' restrictions for a given user? Even straightforward tasks, such as figuring out which Launchpad pages are available for a specific Business role, can become unnecessarily complex.

The IAM Information System App is the comprehensive, user-friendly solution to these challenges. This app is not only intuitive but also robust in its capabilities, streamlining access management and simplifying administrative tasks.

Before exploring the use cases, it is important to note that this application is part of business role template “SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR/BR_APPL_SUP_ENG_DEV_SUP” and accessible through the business catalog business catalogs “SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RA”, “SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM”, “SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM_DISP_PC”, “SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_UM”, “SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_UM_DISP_PC”, and “SAP_A4C_BC_IAM_DISP_PC”.

Now let’s explore its key use cases and discover how this powerful tool can enhance your efficiency and effectiveness as an Administrator.

Case 1: Leveraging the IAM Information System App to Identify Applications Accessible to a Business User

To view the applications accessible to a specific business user, select the “Main Entity” – “Business User”, input the desired 'Business User ID', and click 'Go'. This will display a comprehensive list of applications available to that Business user.

For instance, 889 applications are accessible to the business user CBXXXXXXXXXX.
You also have the option to download an Excel sheet that contains the complete list of applications accessible to a business user.

Similarly, you can view all other items associated with a particular business user, including Business Role, Business Catalog, Restrictions, Launchpad Space, and Launchpad Page.


                                                                            Figure 1: Case 1(a)

Figure 2: Case 1(b)

Case 2: Utilizing the IAM Information App to Identify All Users with Access to a Specific Application

To identify users associated with a specific application, select 'Application' in the main entity filter, enter the application's name in the 'Applications' filter, and click 'Go'. This will display the Business User Names and Business User IDs of all users linked to the application.

For example, searching for the 'Manage Workforce' application reveals all business users with access to it. Additionally, you can download the results in an Excel sheet, as demonstrated in the previous case.

Similarly, you can also view the relationships between this application and the Business Role, Business Catalog, and Business Role Template

                                                                           Figure 3: Case 2

Case 3: Utilizing the IAM App to Identify Restriction Fields with 'Write' Access for a Business user

To identify the restriction fields with 'Write' access for a user, select 'Restriction' in the main entity filter, 'Write' in the access category filter, and the Business User Name in the corresponding field, then click 'Go'. This will display all restriction fields where the user has write access.

For example, in the image below, we can see the restriction fields available for the user 'XXXXXX'.

Figure 4: Case 3

Case 4: Identifying the Pages Accessible to a Specific Business Role

To identify the pages accessible to a specific business user, begin by selecting the 'Launchpad Page' under the 'Main Entity.' Next, choose the relevant 'Business Role' and click 'Go.' This will display all the Launchpad pages associated with that business role. For example, in this case, the 'Production Planner' business role has four Launchpad pages available.

Figure 5: Case 4

Case 5: Evaluating the number of roles or applications associated with a specific Business catalog

To identify the Business roles and applications associated with a specific business catalog, begin by selecting ‘Business Catalog’ under the ‘Main Entity’ field. Enter the desired ‘Business Catalog ID’ and click ‘Go’. The results will display the associated business roles in the first tab and the linked applications in the third tab.
For instance, when analysing the business catalog ‘SAP_FIN_BC_APAR_DP_REQ_PC’, the system displays three business roles—Accounts Payable Accountant, Accounts Receivable Accountant, and Project Manager - Professional Services—in the first tab. Additionally, the third tab lists four associated business applications: Manage Journal Entries - Old Version (Deprecated), Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests, Manage Customer Down Payment Requests, and User Default Parameters. This streamlined process allows for efficient identification of the Business roles and applications tied to a particular Business catalog.

Figure 6: Case 5(a)

Figure 7: Case 5(b)

Case 6: Evaluating the number of roles or applications associated with a specific Business Catalog.

To identify the applications associated with a specific Business Role Template, begin by selecting "Business Role Template" under the "Main Entity" Next, input the desired Business Role Template ID and click "Go." This will display all applications linked to the specified Business Role Template. For instance, in the figure below, the Business Role Template ID “SAP_BR_INVENTORY_MANAGER” was entered, revealing 164 associated applications.

Figure 8: Case 6

There are other opportunities you can take advantage of with this application. I highly recommend giving it a try and exploring all that it has to offer.

Thank you for reading this blog post, hopefully, the blog post was informative and helped you to understand in detail how to use IAM Information System App.

entry.title: need to add additional columns in PM task list component section.
entry.content:
Hello Experts!!

In PM Task list on the component section 

I want to add 3 additional  columns namely:

1)Outline agreement 2)Agreement item 3)Info records (Tab's related SCM - supply chain management )

entry.title: Dual TDS Deduction
entry.content: I want to deduct multiple TDS section in a single AP invoice 94C & 94H in ecommerce invoices please suggest how is it works in SAP B1 ????

entry.title: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content:
I have a managed (self-built) RAP object, that is working pretty well together with the generated Fiori-Elements-App.

Upon creating/updating/deleting a dataset in that BO I want to update another (SAP-built) BO through EML. But as I can't commit changes within the "save_modified" and without committing them they won't get saved, I am a bit lost. I also tried to raise a custom Business-Event, fetching it in another class, yet still EML-Modify plus EML-Commit are crashing on runtime because in the obviously synchronous Event-Handling (why synchronous?) I am still "not allowed" to Commit things.

Hope someone can help me soon on how to solve this, Google was no help at all.

entry.title: EWM Outbound Process – Consolidation of various HUs into one shipping HU (best practice)
entry.content:
Hi SAP EWM Gurus

We have the following requirements:

We pick from different areas into internal boxes (containers with a fixed HU number), which are then sent to the packing area. There the goods are packed into the shipping carton and the shipping HU is created. If there are several internal boxes for one delivery, these should be packed together so that we just send one parcel to the customer.

Our idea was to pick the goods with the RF scanner and then pack them using the Fiori app “Pack Outbound Deliveries”. With the Fiori app, however, we have the problem that we only see one HU at a time (and since we have pre-printed container HUs, we have no barcode with the delivery).

Example select with HU as Reference Number:

Example select with Delivery as Reference Number:

Now I ask myself, what is the best way to do this? Does SAP EWM have a different process in place? Of course, we could develop something, but I think I just miss something.

I am very grateful for any input or process ideas.

Best regards

Simon

entry.title: Understanding SAP Wage Type Mapping
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm seeking information on SAP wage types. Can you guide me on where to find a table, view, report, or configuration that shows how these wage types are mapped to categories such as Total Deductions, Gross Pay, and Basic Pay?

I'm a consultant, not an SAP user, and I'm looking to understand how employee pay elements (wage types) are classified and how salaries are calculated in SAP.

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Streamlining Business Processes with B2B Integration Factory
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business world, effective communication and efficient data exchange between business partners are crucial. The B2B Integration Factory, a powerful solution designed to simplify and streamline B2B interactions, can transform your business processes. 

In one of our recent projects, I delved deep into the possibilities of the B2B Integration Factory and discovered some insights. At first, it can feel like a lot to take in. But once you get the hang of it, you'll see how useful it can be.  

What is B2B Integration Factory? 

The B2B Integration Factory is a service within SAP that enables organizations to manage their B2B processes more efficiently.

The Partner Directory offers centralized partner management, enabling you to store and manage all partner-specific configurations in one place. This ensures consistency and simplifies the maintenance of partner data. 

Additionally, the B2B Integration Factory provides a wide range of predefined integration content, including mappings, schemas, and connectivity options, which significantly reduce implementation time and effort. 

It is built to handle high transaction volumes and can effortlessly scale to meet your business's growing needs. It supports a variety of sender and receiver communication protocols, including AS2, SFTP, HTTP, and also custom options. 

Last but not least, the B2B Integration Factory ensures secure data exchange with features like encryption, digital signatures, and compliance with industry standards such as EDIFACT, X12, TRADACOMS and more 

 Conclusion 

The B2B Integration Factory in SAP makes B2B interactions easier and more efficient. It cuts down on manual work, reduces mistakes, and speeds up transactions. By using ready-made content, it saves on setup and maintenance costs. Plus, with real-time monitoring and analytics, businesses can keep track of data exchanges and stay competitive in today’s connected world. 

If you would like to learn more, please Contact Proclus - www.proclus-group.com

entry.title: reset user password through command line
entry.content:
Hello,

My SAP server works on windows and using S4/Hana DB and I'm trying to reset user's password through command line.

I've found that it is possible by using R3trans command and prepared password_reset.txt file as below;

export
client=400
user=username
password=123456

and run this command;

R3trans -w password_reset.log password_reset.txt

but it gives me this output

4 ETW000 R3trans version 6.26 (release 777 - 19.06.24 - 15:07:06).
4 ETW000 unicode enabled version
4 ETW000 ===============================================
4 ETW000 
4 ETW000 date&time   : 06.12.2024 - 13:31:45
4 ETW000 control file: password_reset.txt
4 ETW000 > export
4 ETW000 > client=400
4 ETW000 > user=SONERCTEST
4 ETW000 > password=123456
4 ETW000 R3trans was called as follows: R3trans -w password_reset.log password_reset.txt
3WETW000 obsolete R3trans option ignored: USER=SONERCTEST (line 3).
4 ETW000 
2EETW000 error when reading the R3trans controlfile
2EETW000 unknown option PASSWORD = yes at line 4, symbol "=".
2EETW000 error when reading the R3trans controlfile
2EETW000 syntax error at line 4, symbol "123456".

What am i doing wrong here?

entry.title: Re: Cannot create single file installer in SAP Installati...
entry.content: Hi, is Version 122 ready to download. I am not to find the new Version with SAP-ME

entry.title: Can someone explain me the Parameter EFB
entry.content:
I am starting with SAP and I have a bit of struggle with understanding exactly how EFB works. I know that this is for giving authorizations or restrictions to users when handling purchasing documents but I do not understand completely what all the fields are or how to start customizing these.

For instance there are the general parameters like:

Display conditions, Enter conditions, W/o Material and then on the right side Fieldselection. I do not understand what this combination generates.

And I will appreciate some guidance as well with the possible reference objects. 

 

Thank you very much for all the people creating such a good learning community!

entry.title: Changelog production order
entry.content:
Hello to everyone,

I tried to find a suitible with searching in the forum. But to be honest I didn't find an answer which fits to me. The answers most were to much in technical details.

To my problem. We are using SAP APO in my company. Week for Week my colleagues in production planning are clearing the production backlog trough rescheduling the start and end dates of the working steps in production oder. So we can see an estimated finish date in the production order. But what is with my original dates when I started the production oder first. Is there an transaction where I can see which starting and enddates were planned before releasing the production oder. I don't only want to know how big is my backlog in hours in total, I also want to know how big is delay in days. Is there a tcode, where I can find this information with the dates or other submenues where I can find it?

 

Thanks in advance.

I would like to know what's the alternate of forms in ABAP Clean Core approach.

entry.title: Re: Background Job output (Report)
entry.content: The maximum width is 1023 characters. What is your report width? It's defined by "LINE-SIZE" in the program introductory statement e.g. https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abapreport_list_options.htm#!ABAP_ADDITION_2@2@

entry.title: Re: ADD CUSTOM FIELDS TO ACCOUNT ASSIGNMENT IN MIGO

entry.title: adobe forms print decimal field in case of initial as empty string

entry.title: Connecting SAP DMS with SAP Cloud Solutions (C4C, ariba etc) to archive documents on On-Prem Archiv
entry.content:
Our goal is to connect various cloud systems to our on-premise archive via the SAP Document Management Service, integration option (DMS) (to store documents). As a first step, we want to integrate the C4C system. Unfortunately, we are having difficulty finding specific documentation for this process. The only documentation we have so far refers to the integration of an S/4 HANA cloud system, but not to C4C or other cloud services. We are looking specifically for C4C and Ariba connection. 

Is there any best practices or guides from SAP that could help the customer to realize the case? 

Any documentation and guidance helps!

entry.title: HELP! The database migration is too slow! #DMOVE2S4
entry.content:
Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition requires data to be moved. In case of your business systems, it might be hundreds of gigabytes or even several terabytes that need to be transferred. Naturally, you want this transfer to be safe and sound – and quick.

This blog collects performance aspects of DMOVE2S4 of Software Update Manager 2.0.

 

Software Update Manager is a tool that offers you a guided and consistent migration to the cloud.

For the migration part, two options are available:

  • DMOVE2S4 – based on Database Migration Option (DMO); recommended
  • DMO with System Move

Both procedures can be combined with a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA or an upgrade of the SAP S/4HANA system.  The DMO of Software Update Manager is a well-established technology, for which you find more information in the following blogs and of course in the official DMO guide available at help.sap.com

  • What is DMO?

Blog: Database Migration Option (DMO) of SUM - Introduction

Blog: DMO: technical background

DMO Guide at help.sap.com: Introduction - Basic Concepts - The DMO Concept

  • What is DMOVE2S4?

Blog: DMOVE2S4 – your one-step approach into the cloud

DMO Guide at help.sap.com: Planning - DMO Move to SAP S/4HANA (on Hyperscaler)

  • What is DMO with System Move?

Blog: DMO with System Move - the use case to change PAS host during DMO

DMO Guide at help.sap.com: Planning - Performing DMO with System Move

 

Which approach should I choose?

When planning a move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, DMOVE2S4 is the recommended approach. It allows downtime-optimization approaches on top (such as downtime-optimized DMO and downtime-optimized Conversion). If the recommended thresholds for network quality (see DMO guide) cannot be met, DMO with System Move is the option of choice. As all data is transferred during downtime, consider using the parallel mode. 

 

DMOVE2S4: Performance aspects of the migration

As we have seen, the safe and sound part is covered – your data is moved by a well-established, proven tool with thousands of successful executions. However, what needs to be monitored individually in every case, is the duration of the migration and the related downtime of the system.

If you are facing long migration phases, consider the following aspects:

 

1️⃣   Network quality

All your data needs to be moved from point A to B. If you network only allows to transfer 1GB/hour and you need to transfer 120GB, it is physically not possible to be faster than 120 hours, regardless of how we tweak the Software Update Manager. 

Blog: DMO = Do Math Obligations!

The Software Update Manager documents the network quality in several log files. As of Software Update Manager 2.0 SP21:

PHASE_DBSTAT.LOG / PHASE_DBSTAT.SAV ➡️latency of the connection
*RUN files ➡️speed and latency statistics of the data transfer

⚠️Especially the *RUN files are extremely useful to identify potential bottlenecks in import and / or export.



2️⃣  Optimizing the migration process with statistics in duration files

The good news is: Software Update Manager is equipped with logic to minimize that tail (i.e. lone tables increasing the overall runtime.

Blog: DMO: background on table split mechanism

It’s first calculation is sufficient most of the time – but you can improve it with feeding the actual runtimes of the previous run to the tool. For more details, please refer to:

DMO Guide at help.sap.com: Planning - Performance Optimization with Migration Duration File

 

3️⃣  Parameters

The easiest influence you have on the procedure is the number of r3load processes you configure. Please note, that more is not automatically better – refer to the blog about table splits from point 2. If you want to optimize the number of parallel r3loads, check out point 4.

 

4️⃣  Fine-tuning with Benchmarking and migration repetition

With the Benchmarking Tool of Software Update Manager, you can jump directly to the migration (without stack.xml). It also allows to select only a part of the data (e.g. largest 10% of tables) for migration. In addition, the migration repetition option of Software Update Manager allows you to rerun the downtime (and change parameters). Combining these two allows you to tweak your migration without having to rerun the whole Software Update Manager procedure.

For more information, please refer to:

Blog: DMO: introducing the benchmarking tool

Blog: Optimizing DMO Performance

DMO Guide at help.sap.com: Migration Tools - Benchmark Migration

DMO Guide at help.sap.com: Running the Software Update Manager - Special Features for DMO - Using the Migration Repetition Option for Testing

 

5️⃣  Downtime-optimized DMO

One of the easiest options to reduce the downtime is the downtime-optimized DMO. With this approach, part of the migration is done during uptime.

Blog: DMO: downtime optimization by migrating app tables during uptime

The initial selection from Software Update Manager depends on the stack. To tailor the tables that are migrated partly in uptime to your needs, Software Update Manager Toolbox allows you to select tables for uptime migration.

SAP Note: 3092738 - Software Update Manager Toolbox - Central SAP Note

⚠️Please note that rules apply to which tables can be transferred partly on uptime, such as having a primary key on database level (see SAP Note 2547309). Additionally, the data transfer in uptime via database triggers introduces additional needs for resources. Consider the Impact Analysis delivered in Software Update Manager Toolbox to estimate the required space.

 

❗  For the experts: Manual intervention

If you have applied all measures above and still see a tail in execution, you might want to manually split a specific table or change the order via priority.

SAP Note: 3386079 - SUM DMO scenarios: Manual Options for Table Splitting and Table Migration

⚠️ Please note that this is not necessary in most of the cases. If you observe a fundamental miscalculation by Software Update Manager leading to a tail, feel free to contact us via ticket on component BC-UPG-TLA.

 

 

While some of the information shared here dates back to 2015, it remains highly relevant and valuable for your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The goal of this post was to bring all these insights together and paint a cohesive picture that will support your migration.

All the best!

Mirja Kempin

Software Update Manager Product Management

entry.title: Re: SAP Plant maintenance Guide
entry.content:
Hi @Seydouba 
I am not quite sure if you are looking for learning materials or for documentation.

For SAP documentation please vist our SAP Help Portal:
for the topic you are looking for the Plant Maintenance (PM)  might be helpful.
There you can find the single roles for the "Plant Maintenance (PM)" component.

For learning materials please visit  SAP Learning there you can find everything you need to learn, innovate, and achieve more with free and premium SAP Learning resources.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Product Compliance, SDS Shipment goes to "Co...
entry.content:
Hi

#1: The output of safety data sheet considers the “output parameter determination” rules maintained in your system. If the execution of the rule results in output not required, all SDS shipment requests will not be output. Please check the defined rules for the application SAFETY_DATA_SHEET in the Output Parameter Determination SAP Fiori app.

#2: Most likely is the setup incomplete if output requests remain in status in preparation. You can press the link “output details” in the shipment monitor app and check the messages there. The central setup guide of Output Management is available here: Output Management Setup Guide (1LQ).

Kind regards,
Mathias

entry.title: Where can I start learning the basics of SAP Global Trade Services?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring and Using Classifications for International Trade learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will give you a broader and deeper insight into classification for both customs and compliance purposes.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to map various nomenclatures for international trade in SAP GTS and to classify products making full use of the functions SAP GTS provides.

Prerequisites

  • Learning Journey Discovering the Basics of SAP Global Trade Services

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Mismatch between IBP WebUI and Excel Add in
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Have an observation in Quality environment. The data seen via Excel Add in say 'Consensus Demand' does not get reflected via WebUI app 'Projected Stock' . Have seen this discrepancy for quite a few materials. Any pointers as to what needs to be taken care of for these views to be in synch?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to change bsik-xesrd.
entry.content:
This flag should be updated by standard as soon as either field BSEG-ESRNR or ESRRE would be filled with non initial value

Look for

  • FM such as FI_DOCUMENT_CHANGES or FI_ITEMS_MASS_CHANGE
  • BDC on FB02, FB09

In most cases those fields must be allowed to change (Customizing transaction OB32)

Last try could be to add a "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true, to each block... maybe that avoids the overwriting...

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to use SAP Returnable Packaging Management?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Managing Basic Business Scenarios in SAP Returnable Packaging Management learning journey.

Overview
Get ready for an enlightening journey into SAP Returnable Packaging Management, SAP's industry cloud solution. Learn to manage returnable packaging processes across the supply chain, collaborate effectively with suppliers and customers, and gain hands-on experience with SAP's tools. Enhance your skills in defining key terms, understanding ownership scenarios, and configuring master data efficiently.

Learning objectives

  • Describe and execute the process of managing returnable packaging materials with suppliers and customers.
  • ​Understand the required master data and configuration within SAP Returnable Packaging Management​.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Is there an OData API available for performing BIN Sorting via API?
entry.content:
We are unable to identify an API that would allow us to perform BIN sorting. We need this functionality as part of managing the data lifecycle for S4HPC in a side-by-side application in the BTP ABAP environment.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there an OData API available for performing BIN So...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, there is no specific API available for performing BIN sorting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud as part of managing data lifecycle for a side-by-side application in the Business Technology Platform (BTP) ABAP environment. However, as an alternative, you can consider creating custom logic in your ABAP code to perform the BIN sorting operation.

Please check the documents below for further information:

Storage Bin Sorting

Operations for Warehouse Storage Bin API   (Only available to Retrieve the Warehouse Storage Bin)

Storage Bin

 

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Not able to make the New column to show in front scre...
entry.content: Hi , Thanks for the reply, Please let me know where to check that ? Do you mean metadata cds view ?

entry.title: Improved Integration with Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
As a customer you may need to work with more than one type of SAP BTP applications, like Integration Suite, SAP Mobile services or SAP Build apps. In such a scenario, you might have a requirement to use either a different SAP Cloud Transport Management service dedicated to a landscape for a particular SAP BTP application or choose a different transport route as you create different transport requests.

Content Agent is a tool that acts as a local agent and operates at sub account level for managing content operations for different SAP BTP services. This service acts as a technical enabler to integrate with Cloud Transport Management by providing option to transport the enterprise ready changes across SAP BTP accounts following standardised change management policies.

With the recent improvements in SAP Content Agent, it is possible to have more flexibility in integrating with Cloud Transport Management. Additionally when the transport request is created, you can also monitor the status and logs at the target node level.

Subscription to Content Agent service

To use SAP Content Agent you will need a subscription to "free" plan (saas application) which is free and auto entitled in every SAP BTP account. Along with that you will need some roles assigned to your user for which you can use pre-defined role collections. For more information, See Subscribe the UI plan and Assign User Roles and Permissions.
To enable the supported SAP BTP applications, where destination configuration is required, please refer to Supported Transport Scenarios and Supported Content Types.

Subscription to Cloud Transport Management

As a prerequisite you must have a subscription to Cloud Transport Management service and landscape configuration by creating target nodes and routes.
For more details see Subscribing to Cloud Transport Management and Configuring the Landscape.
To export content to the transport queue of Cloud Transport Management you need a destination in the source (development) SAP BTP account, see Create TransportManagementService Destination

Integration options

While launching the SAP Content Agent service, the overview page is loaded. Click on the "Cloud Transport Management" tile to see the different options and connectivity status. 

  • Destination - Select a destination from the list of available destinations targeted to Cloud Transport Management. The list will provide the destinations created in the BTP account having the prefix "TransportManagementService". By default, the destination is selected as TransportManagementService.
    For more information, see Create TransportManagementService Destination

  • Connection Status - Displays the integration status between Content Agent and Cloud Transport Management

  • Error Status - Displays the connection error details and possible workaround to restore connection between Content Agent and Cloud Transport Management.

  • Source Node - Select the source node of the transport route. You can select source node depending on the specific applications to separate transport requests based on content types.

    Note: If Source Node is not selected, the sourceSystemId property configured in the destination is used as default.

  • Go to SAP Cloud Transport Management - Launches SAP Cloud Transport Management Home screen.

View Transport Request status and logs

Once you have created the transport request using the export wizard, you can view the information of past activities performed using Content Agent UI.

entry.title: Re: Troubles connecting to HANA Cloud from BAS
entry.content:
The error message you attached shows an authentication error and the correlation-id.

You (or your DB admin) should check a detailed reason when querying your database with

select * from AUTHENTICATION_ERROR_DETAILS

like:

entry.title: Where can I learn the basic configuration settings for delivery documents in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Delivery Processing in SAP S/4HANA Sales learning journey.

Overview
In this learning journey, you can learn the basic configuration settings for delivery documents in SAP S/4HANA. Both inbound and outbound processes are discussed, as well as the integration with SAP EWM.

Learning objectives
This learning journey will prepare you to:

  • Work with delivery documents
  • Maintain organizational units for delivery processing
  • Control deliveries (such as the delivery type and delivery item category determination)
  • Post a goods issue for a delivery
  • Integrate delivery processing with EWM

 

Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Sales 
    or 
  • Applying Supply Chain Execution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Hana express starting problem
entry.content:
I'm trying to start the saplabs/hanaexpress:latest container on macOS(M3 processor and Sequoia 15.1.1). The container is stuck at the message: Waiting for HANA to finish start up ... .

 

I have read the description of the image https://hub.docker.com/r/saplabs/hanaexpress but two things to mention.

Firstly the provided description is for linux OS and secondly there are configs like step: Edit the host sysctl.conf file where such directory does not exist on mac. DBeaver can connect to it but is this message okey or how can i do it to work properly. Because the next step is to be used with TestContainers library.


This is the command which I have used to start it.

sudo docker run -d -p 39013:39013 -p 39017:39017 -p 39041-39045:39041-39045 -p 1128-1129:1128-1129 -p 59013-59014:59013-59014 -v /Users/{IUSER}/Downloads/hana_express:/hana/mounts \

--ulimit nofile=1048576:1048576 \

--sysctl kernel.shmmax=1073741824 \

--sysctl net.ipv4.ip_local_port_range='40000 60999' \

--sysctl kernel.shmall=8388608 \

--name hana \

saplabs/hanaexpress:latest \

--passwords-url file:///hana/mounts/pass.json \

--agree-to-sap-license

entry.title: Re: ADD CUSTOM FIELDS TO ACCOUNT ASSIGNMENT IN MIGO
entry.content: How to do a modification of standard? Do the modification of the standard dynpro directly.

entry.title: What you think about code plagiarism?
Re: Crystal Reports: nvarchar(max) fields truncating after 255 characters in runtime
entry.content:
What version of the SDK is the app using? Did you save the Report after updating and Verifying the Database in CR Designer?

CR saves the DB client used in the RPT file so the SDK will use it.

If the report is not updated, saved, and it can't find the old Client CR will default to a generic database client SQL which may not be capable of using that field type.

entry.content:

I am facing problem while creating STO, in pricing, it says: Condition Record Ignored (Requirement not fulfilled), though the condition record is properly maintained using MEK1. The problem is persistent only for a specific material, while for other materials the pricing is correctly assigned in conditions.

 

Please help

 

Thanks for your urgent help

entry.title: Is it a good decision for someone without an IT background to learn SAP Security GRC?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have 5.3 years of experience working in an MNC in a non-IT role (BPO), and I am now looking to shift my career towards IT, specifically in SAP Security GRC, for better growth opportunities. I am not interested in programming languages, and have found limited opportunities in my current field.

I would greatly appreciate insights from individuals who have transitioned into SAP Security GRC from non-IT backgrounds or from those currently working in this domain. Is it a good decision to pursue SAP Security GRC for long-term career growth?

Additionally, I would like to know:

1. What is the average salary package for someone with 3 to 3.5 years of experience in SAP Security GRC?
    Are there any current job openings for candidates with this level of experience? What is the demand for            SAP Security GRC professionals in the market?
2. Are there opportunities for freshers in this field?
3. Could you recommend any reputable training institutes in Hyderabad for SAP Security GRC with contacts?
4. Is certification required after completing the course, and if so, what is the cost?
5. Is any programming knowledge (like Java, Python, etc.) required to learn SAP Security GRC? Is it a "no            coding" field?
6. Is certification necessary to get job calls and Interviews? If so, what is the certification fee?

Looking forward to your valuable feedback.

Regards,
Sridhar.

entry.title: Re: Zero Total Value on PR for Limit Orders in SAP Ariba...
entry.content:
Hello,

Have not seen this before. It could possibly be due to some customization. Try once with different data and different sequence of operations. Also, deactivating customizations and trying once could help to analyze further.

entry.title: Is it a good idea to learn SAP Security GRC without an IT background? Any suggestions?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have 5.3 years of experience working in a non-IT role (BPO) at an MNC, and I am now looking to transition into IT for better career growth, specifically in SAP Security GRC. Unfortunately, I have not found many opportunities in my current field, and I am not interested in programming languages.

I would appreciate advice from those who have moved from non-IT to SAP Security GRC or are currently working in this field. Is learning SAP Security GRC a good decision for long-term career growth?

Additionally, I have the following questions:

1. Is any programming knowledge (like Java, Python, etc.) required to learn SAP Security GRC? Is it a "no            coding" field?
2. What is the average salary for someone with 3 to 3.5 years of experience in SAP Security GRC? Are there        openings for candidates with this experience, and what is the current demand?
3. Are there opportunities available for freshers in SAP Security GRC?
4. Could you recommend any good training institutes in Hyderabad for SAP Security GRC?
5. Is certification necessary to get job interviews and calls? If so, what is the certification fee?

Thank you for your time and insights!

Best regards,
KSR.

entry.title: Re: PM Order operation list
entry.content:
Greetings,

there isn't any standard way to transform notification tasks to work order operations. You would need to develop your custom logic. There is s standard scenario where a notification also holds information regarding the task list (group and group counter) but this information is static since it is derived from existing master data.

Perhaps one way would be to maintain a general task list with all possible operations - you could call this a super task list (nomenclature from variant configuration ^^). In next step you would need to maintain a mapping table which would include tasks from catalogue type 2 mapped to operations from this super task list.  

When you create an order from a notification you then take the tasks from notification and operations from the super task list  to determine the operations on the maintenance work order.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: How to Edit Multiple Components Simultaneously in SAP Build Apps’ Logic Canvas?
entry.content:
Does anyone know if it’s possible to edit the properties of multiple components at once in the SAP Build Apps logic canvas? For instance, the "Hide component" and "Show component" flow functions are great for handling a single component individually, but it would be extremely helpful if we could adjust the visibility of multiple elements simultaneously.

I tried creating a new flow function from an existing component and even managed to get it to accept two component IDs. However, I couldn’t create more than one input property, which limited the functionality and prevented simultaneous actions on more than one component.

Has anyone faced this situation before, or does anyone have a workaround or alternative solution to overcome this limitation?
@Dan_Wroblewski, could you help me with this issue?

Regards,

Swaroop

entry.title: Re: Subcomponent of subcontracted material (main material) is not showing in MD4C

entry.title: Exchanging SAP IBP data using the native Snowflake adapter in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
In the previous Blogs, I explained the steps needed for exchanging data between SAP IBP and Snowflake. We used the Open connectors platform for creating an instance of the Snowflake and AWS S3 connectors. In this blog, I explain how we can exchange data with Snowflake using the native connectors from SAP Cloud Integration directly. You can find more details of this new adapter in these pages :- SAP Business Accelerator Hub - Snowflake Adapter

Fig.1 High level overview of components.
Prerequisites

Install the new Snowflake adapter in your SAP Cloud Integration tenant. For this,

  1. go to Discover workspace in the SAP Cloud Integration.
  2. In the Search box look for Snowflake adapter for SAP Integration Suite package. Select and copy that package into your Design Workspace.
  3. In your Design workspace, select the Snowflake adapter. On the Actions tab of the selection, click Deploy.

This would deploy the adapter in your tenant, and you would be able to use it in your iFlows later.  You can monitor the deployment in the Monitor – Integration and APIs – Overview page under Manager Integration Content.

The Snowflake adapter is designed to work as a receiver adapter. An Iflow in SAP Cloud Integration would initiate a call to the adapter. The adapter uses JDBC as a transport protocol and uses the database credentials from Snowflake. The adapter also uses 3 different external staging options – Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage and Microsoft Azure. In my previous blog, I explained how we use Amazon S3 as a staging area. We use the same option in this approach.

We also have an Amazon Web Services adapter available natively in SAP Cloud Integration. This adapter supports operations with S3 Buckets. To use this you have to deploy the adapter in your tenant, like you did with the Snowflake adapter.  

  1. go to Software Center - SAP for Me. -> SUPPORT PACKAGES & PATCHES -> By Alphabetical Index (A-Z) -> C -> SAP CP IS ADAPTER ADVANCE PACK & SAP CP IS ADAPTER BASE PACK: SAP CPIS AWS ADAPTER.
  2. If you are using an SAP Cloud Integration version which has a Camel 3x version, use the ZIP file which has Camel 3x as suffix.
  3. Download and unzip the file to a folder you can remember. In the Integration Suite -> Design -> create a new Package for example – AWS Adapter Package. Switch to edit mode and the under the Artifacts tab clock on Add.
  4. In the new pop-up screen, select Add Integration Adapter and select the .ESA file from the adapter folder of your unzip from the previous step.
  5. On the Actions tab, click Deploy.

This would deploy the adapter in your tenant. This adapter helps you to work with many Amazon web services, S3 being one among them. We reuse the Object Store credentials from our previous blog in this one too.

  • This means, you would have created two aliases based on the Object store credentials, one for the AWS Key ID and the other for the AWS Secret Key. For details on how to create these credentials – take a look at this help page. If you are doing it for the first time,
    1. Open the Security Material app from the Monitor -> Integrations and APIs menu item. Create a new Secure Parameter with the name:- AWS_S3_Access_Key_Allias. You can also create a name on your wish.
    2. In the Secure parameter input box, paste the access_key_id from the credentials JSON you get while configuring the Keys for Object store for your AWS S3 bucket.
    3. Do the same for storing the corresponding secret access key also as a secure parameter.

Usage in iFlows

When you write data to Snowflake with data from SAP IBP, you can perform the needed transformation to create a JSON file that can be stored in the AWS S3 staging bucket. The below snippet can help you with a format.

 

try{
        JSONArray results_array = new JSONArray();  
        def count = 1;
        JSONArray valueJSONArray = input.get("IBPReadKeyFigures").get("item");
        if(valueJSONArray.length() > 0) {
            
            def ibp_data = valueJSONArray.collect{[
                "PRODUCT" : it.PRDID,
                "CUSTOMER" : it.CUSTID,
                "LOCATION" : it.LOCID,
                "UNITS" : it.UOMID,
                "COMMENTS" : "From IBP - Count",
                "CONSDEMAND" : it.ACTUALSQTY as BigDecimal,
                "KEYFIGUREDATE" : it.TSTFR
                ]
            }
            
            def builder = new JsonBuilder();
            def result_array_data = []
            
            Integer counterValue = message.getProperty('OverallLogCounter') as Integer ?:0;
            counterValue = counterValue + 1;
            
            def root = builder ibp_data        	
            def prettyBody = builder.toPrettyString();
    	    long len = prettyBody.getBytes().length; 
            String payloadSize = len.toString(); 
        	
            // CLEAR and SET HTTP HEaders 
            message.setHeader("Content-Length", payloadSize);         	
            message.setBody(prettyBody);        	
        }  
        
    } catch (JSONException e) { 
	message.setBody(e);
    }

 

In the above code – valueJSONArray would have the data from SAP IBP in JSON format. We just format this to a JSON array with column names as defined in the Snowflake database schema. JsonBuilder can be used to convert the JSON array to a string that can be sent to the AWS S3 Bucket.

Staging bucket configuration

Create a new local process which can be used to stage the data in an AWS S3 bucket.

  1. Create a Request-Reply combination. In the Adapter type select – AmazonWebServices, which you have deployed. Select the Message Protocol as S3.
  2. In the Connections tab of the adapter – enter a name for the Bucket, Access Key Alias and the Secret Key Alias (for the last two, use the name you had given for storing the Key and Secret as secure parameters in the previous section (Step 6).

Fig.2 Amazon S3 connection configuration.

In the Processing tab, select the S3 Operation as Write. You can also mention a directory name as well as a file name. These details can be provided as configuration parameters too. You can also provide the content type as application/json. Additional options for file handling are also available, it can be used as required.

Fig.3 Amazon S3 bucket processing configuration.

Upsert to Snowflake

Create a new local process to copy data from the staging bucket to the actual database in Snowflake. For this,

  1. Create a Request-Reply combination. Select the adapter as Snowflake. Make sure the message protocol is JDBC.
  2. In the Connections tab of the adapter, enter the JDBC user credentials name. This can be created as user credentials in the Manage Security Material section.
  3. In the Address section, enter the URI of your database. Usually it is jdbc❄️//{your-tenant-name}.snowflakecomputing.com.
  4. Enter the values for the Database, Schema and Warehouse fields. You can make all of these as configuration parameters.
  5. In the Processing tab, select the operation as “Bulk Upsert”. Also provide a valid table name from Snowflake.
  6. Select the Staging Location as “Amazon S3”, Access type as “External Stage” and also give the name of your stage definitions in Snowflake.
  7. In the Optional parameters – you can provide the following:- match_by_column_name='CASE_INSENSITIVE' file_format =  (TYPE = 'JSON'  STRIP_OUTER_ARRAY = TRUE). This is to make sure that the column names are case considered in any format type. We have to strip off the outer JSON key to insert this data.

The configured adapter would look something like this,

Fig.4 Snowflake adapter configuration.

Once these local sub processes are completed, you can call them from a main flow like the below screenshot. A step-by-step guide was presented in the TechEd 2024 virtual event. You can find the exercises as well as the sample iFlow in this Github repo. 

Fig.5 High level overview of iFlow components.

Conclusion

In this method, we explained how the native adapters for Snowflake and Amazon Web Services can be deployed, configured and used for exchanging data with SAP IBP. We did observe there is a slight improvement in the performance with this approach compared to the previous one using Open Connectors Platform. This is because a roundtrip to the OCP is avoided by the native adapters.

entry.title: SAP EPPM public cloud
entry.content: 1- SAP EPPM public cloud Field requirement and the Notification possibilities

entry.title: Re: sapui5 vscode problem when preview - component not found
entry.content: @ future readers; please see the same question asked at https://stackoverflow.com/q/79247043/5846045.

entry.title: Re: Can someone explain me the Parameter EFB
entry.content:
Hello @miguelfdzs 

First, you need to customize authorizations for buyers in the configuration path Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Authorization Management -> Define Function Authorizations for Buyers -> Function Authorizations: Purchase Order:

The settings of an authorization are quite self-evident. There is also F1 help for each field.

Each authorization is given a 2 chars code. This code is then assigned to a user in the SU01 transaction (tab "Parameters") with the parameter id EFB.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SOAP Sales order API - data type enhancetype
entry.content: There's no screenshot. NB: just copy/paste the screenshot inside the question, i.e. make it visible, don't link it as an attachment.

entry.title: Re: Regex help
entry.content:
Hi @aravind_indu ,

Have you managed to make it work maybe with the regexp?
Meaning that my answer is a solution? 

Best,
Peter

entry.title: On-Premise AI Excellence: How SAP HANA EE and Skybuffer AI Deliver
entry.content:
All right, AI stories are nice and interesting; however, as SAP posted this week, “Today’s geopolitical environment is defined by uncertainty.” This is moving us toward the necessity of controlling innovative AI solutions in disconnected-from-Internet cases.

Can an Enterprise-Level AI Solution Run In Your Datacenter?
Of course, no problem at all! Let’s think a bit—there is an awesome SAP HANA Enterprise Edition available that has an embedded applications layer, and, in fact, this is a Cloud Foundry solution with all necessary enterprise security mechanisms, all delivered by SAP.

Fig. 1 Skybuffer AI Solution Architecture 

Great! So, we have the following on-premise ingredients:

  • SAP Cloud Foundry Cockpit
  • SAP HANA Database
  • SAP Applications Runtime
  • SAP Job Scheduling

These ingredients allow us to develop SAP Fiori applications using XSA technology directly on the SAP HANA EE application layer.

What is missing?
In a general sense, a few things are missing in SAP HANA EE on-premise to act as your enterprise AI on-premise platform:

  • SAP HANA Vector Engine for document information processing (RAG scenarios)
  • SAP AI Core
  • Action Server (no-code SAP Fiori applications allowing AI modeling)

Is the on-premise gap closed?
Yes! Starting in September 2024, our Skybuffer AI partner solution, developed on SAP technologies, successfully passed the pilot stage and is now available in the SAP Store in both versions:

  • SAP BTP Deployment
  • SAP HANA EE (on-premise) Deployment

We have brought the following missing parts to SAP HANA EE on-premise:

  • SAP HANA Partner Vector Engine
  • AI Core (de facto your preferred on-premise LLM model, for example, Llama 3.1 8B, running inside the SAP Applications Layer in Docker)
  • A set of SAP Fiori applications for no-code AI modeling

 Fig. 2 Skybuffer AI Modeling Dashboard

As a result, you get a ready-to-use platform that integrates Generative AI, RAG, and Conversational AI, allowing businesses to automate tasks quickly and efficiently. It saves costs, reduces development complexity, and accelerates time to value. Everything is on-premise on SAP HANA EE (which you can host even under the table 🙂 ).

Cherry on the cake!
Skybuffer AI is out-of-the-box connected to SAP ECC and SAP S4HANA on-premise systems. We have already developed approximately 400 different integrated business scenarios that allow you to use secure AI assistants connected to your SAP ECC and SAP S4HANA via single sign-on. Yes, another magic—no need to adjust roles and profiles for your users; they get in chat exactly the same information they are authorized to see in the SAP system.

Fig. 3 SAP ECC or SAP S4HANA in Microsoft Teams Channel

What channels are covered?
Communication can be done through many channels, the most popular are:

  • Microsoft Teams
  • Web Chat component (can be embedded in any web page)
  • Zoom
  • SAP Fiori Web Chat

Everything is just a configuration on the Skybuffer AI platform (on your premises)—no need for Python, Node.js, or any other type of development.

What about AI Agents?
Sure, those nice 24/7 employees are also here! Any AI model from Skybuffer AI can be scheduled to execute monitoring, document processing, or any other task. Each time an AI Agent runs, you see the execution results in the conversation log because the AI Agent uses generative AI to explain what has been done and tag the log.

 Fig. 4 AI Agent Generative Log

You can chat with the AI Agent anytime to check the heartbeat of the process. Is it not magic?

Fig. 5 AI Agents Status Check

What about RAG document processing?
Here, we not only supply an RAG solution that is ready to consume without any single line of code. Skybuffer AI also has an RAG Document Viewer that allows your employees to see the documents currently accessible by RAG, check document content, and upload new documents directly from the desktop.

 Fig. 6 Skybuffer AI - RAG Document Management

All documents have metadata parameters, so you can classify documents using 48 out-of-the-box string, numbering, and date-type parameters. What you need to do is define your aliases for parameters, so the RAG Fiori application will speak your company’s terms.

What about Joule?
Let’s wait for Q1 2025. Once SAP makes Joule Build available for SAP Partners, we will connect the Skybuffer AI platform to be available via the Joule communication channel as well. In other words, we complement Joule by bringing Skybuffer AI skills directly into Joule’s embedded AI skills.

Interesting and exciting? Agree!

What do you need to run enterprise-level AI on-premise?
Only SAP HANA, which you most probably already have!

Fig. 7 Skybuffer AI - Action Server for no-code AI Modeling

Performance?
Hmmm, that is something really cool. Old, nice on-premise performance, finally 🙂

entry.title: Re: preventive maintenance plan hierarchical strategies
entry.content:
Greetings,

it would appear so yes. I have prepared an example of two maintenance plans for with the same maintenance strategy.

Strategy contains two packages: 

I have prepared three different tasks lists:

  • FIRST: Contains two operations - one for 1Y package and other for 5Y package
  • SECOND: Contains one operation for 1Y package
  • THIRD: Contains one operation for 5Y package

Operations were properly assigned to packages - below example for FIRST task list example.

I prepared a maintenance plan for first scenario - one maintenance item with a task list containing two operations. Scheduling result is visible below.

I prepared a maintenance plan for second scenario - two maintenance items. Each with a different task list. First with one operation assigned to 1Y package and second with one operation assigned to 5Y package.

Scheduling result is visible below.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: How to update a RAP object within another RAP-object?
entry.content: when the calling side is done, the commit is issued by the framework, the called bo should be saved aslo. how you test your scenario?

entry.title: Upgrade SAP S/4HANA from 2021 to 2023 FPS02: Wrong Fiori Version
entry.content:
Good Morning,

I have recently upgraded a S/4HANA system from 2021 to 2023 FPS02.

After the Upgrade, I noticed the following:
SAP FIORI FES FOR S/4HANA 2023 SP01 was chosen in the stack by the MP, instead of SP02
I had to update UIBAS001 758 from SP01 to SP02 by using K-75802INUIBAS001.SAR.

What is all the more surprising is that Note 3340880 describes that FIORI FES FOR S/4HANA is comprised of SAP_UI 758 and UI for Basis Applications 758.

SAP recommends that SPS should be implement to warranty Stack integrity.
But, the Maintenance Planner has taken SP2 for SAP_UI and SP1 for INUIBAS.

Would you know why the maintenance planner has chosen SP01 instead of SP02 for UI for the target Basis Applications 758?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning - Unable to add Value to a Parent Node
entry.content: Thank you @thomasboub. Selecting "Reason for unplannable data" showed that the account type of leaf nodes and the parent node are not the same. Infact, an account type was not given for the parent node in the master data table. Thank you, the issue is resolved now.

entry.title: Re: The use of Class CL_APS_BC_SCOPE_CHANGE_API is not permitted.
entry.content: I am facing same issue.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse - Where-Used functionality for ABAP CDS views...
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you tried to follow the instructions in SAP Note 28022 to create the index for the where-used list of SAP objects? This might solve the issue.

Kind regards,
Armin

entry.title: The Art and Science of CAP (part 5) with Daniel Hutzel
entry.content:
Daniel Hutzel continues on his exploration of The Art and Science of CAP, sharing insights and ideas from the origins, philosophy and magic of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. #SAPCAP #TASC

Watch the previous episodes via this playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAe45xlhIfhTYB9G0mdRVjI

entry.title: Variable size item as a by-product, negative qty in BOM
entry.content:
We produce steel components from steel plates project wise. For that reason we want to create WBS BOM for the project. The input for that WBS BOM is a tool that calculates how many whole plates we have to put in and it also calculates how much of the whole plate we get back as a reusable rectangle (as a by-product). In the BOM we would like to put in whole plates as pieces (stock item L)  and what we get back as the same material with a usable part of the plate remaining as a variable size item with a negative quantity (by-product), but this variable size item doesn't allow negative quantities. Also in customizing trying to set item category R for variable size item  to both negative and positive quantity, but then SAP issues an error 29617 that a VSItm should always be positive

Is there a way to change the error 29617 to a warning either via customizing or via a BADI / user-exit?

Or is there any other suggestion how to solve this?
